title: Export Best Model Configuration
goal: Allow the user to select one deployment as the 'Best' model after viewing comparison results, and export its configuration as a downloadable JSON file containing endpoint, deployment name, model metadata, and a timestamp — without the API key.

in_scope: Add a 'Select Best Model' radio button or dropdown below the results section
Populate the selector with deployment names from the current results
Generate a JSON object with: endpoint, deployment_name, api_type ('azure_foundry'), inference_type, model_name, pricing_reference, export_timestamp
Provide the JSON as a downloadable file via Streamlit's st.download_button()
Ensure the API key is never included in the exported JSON
out_of_scope: Exporting multiple model configurations at once
Saving export history or logging exports
Encrypting or signing the export file

inputs: Comparison results from session state (from Part 07)
Config endpoint from session state (from Part 02)
Deployment metadata from session state (from Part 03)
PRD section 4 — Export best model configuration
outputs: src/app.py — updated with best model selection UI and export download button
dependencies: Part 07 — results must be available in session state
checklist: Create src/export.py with generate_export_config(endpoint, deployment) function
Build the export dict with keys: endpoint, deployment_name, api_type, inference_type, model_name, pricing_reference, export_timestamp
Set inference_type from the deployment's model_type (e.g., 'chat')
Set pricing_reference to the model_name (for pricing lookup reference)
Set export_timestamp to current UTC ISO 8601 timestamp
Explicitly verify the API key is NOT included in the output dict
Return the dict serialized as a formatted JSON string
In src/app.py, add a section below results: 'Select Best Model'
Render st.radio() or st.selectbox() with deployment names from successful results only
Render 'Export Configuration' download button using st.download_button()
On click, call generate_export_config() and provide the JSON string as file content
acceptance_criteria: The best model selector only appears after results are displayed
Only successfully completed deployments appear in the selector (not errored ones)
Clicking Export Configuration downloads a valid JSON file
The downloaded JSON contains all required fields: endpoint, deployment_name, api_type, inference_type, model_name, pricing_reference, export_timestamp
The downloaded JSON does NOT contain an api_key field
export_timestamp is a valid ISO 8601 UTC timestamp
The export function is testable independently of Streamlit
review_focus: Confirm the API key cannot leak through any code path in the export module
The export JSON structure matches the PRD specification exactly
The selector filters out failed deployments
Download button uses appropriate MIME type (application/json)
Timestamp uses UTC and ISO 8601 format
risks_or_notes: If all deployments failed, the best model section should not render (no options available)
The export_timestamp should reflect the export time, not the inference time
Consider adding the prompt text to the export in future iterations (not MVP scope)
st.download_button() in Streamlit triggers a rerun — ensure session state is preserved
definition_of_done: The export JSON structure contains all 7 required fields
API key is provably absent from exported data
Best model selection UI renders after results
Download button produces a valid, parsable JSON file
End-to-end flow: compare → select best → export works in the browser
